summary: Per-Coach Ownership Boundaries Testing Complete - 24/25 backend tests pass (96%), frontend ownership filtering verified. One critical bug found in /api/advocacy/recommendations for coach users (500 error).

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error for coach users. Root cause: filter_by_athlete_id() in advocacy.py is called on dict (from list_recommendations) instead of a list. The function iterates over dict keys (strings) causing AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'get'.
critical.fix_suggestion: filter_by_athlete_id() needs to filter each list inside the dict (needs_attention, drafts, recently_sent, closed) separately, not the dict itself

ui_bugs.issue: Shows 'Failed to load recommendations' error toast and 'No recommendations found' for coach users due to backend 500 error

action_items: FIX: /app/backend/routers/advocacy.py line 29 - filter_by_athlete_id must filter each list inside the dict returned by list_recommendations(), not the dict itself
critical_code_review_comments: ownership.py: Well-designed ownership service with proper caching and clear API (get_visible_athlete_ids, can_access_athlete, filter_by_athlete_id, filter_events_by_ownership)
athletes.py: Properly implements 403 boundary check using can_access_athlete() for individual athlete access and notes
support_pods.py: Properly implements 403 boundary check for Support Pod access
mission_control.py: Correctly filters alerts, signals, events and computes coach-specific snapshot
events.py: Correctly filters events by athlete_ids using filter_events_by_ownership()
advocacy.py: BUG - filter_by_athlete_id called on dict instead of list items. list_recommendations() returns {needs_attention: [], drafts: [], ...} but filter expects a list

success_rate.backend: 96% (24/25 tests passed, 1 xfail for known bug)
success_rate.frontend: 90% (Ownership filtering works, Advocacy page blocked by backend bug)

seed_data_creation: None - used existing seeded athletes with primary_coach_id assignments
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ownership boundaries implemented and working for all endpoints EXCEPT /api/advocacy/recommendations which has a type mismatch bug. Director sees 25 athletes, Coach Williams sees 13 (odd-numbered), Coach Garcia sees 12 (even-numbered). The bug is in advocacy.py where filter_by_athlete_id() is called on the dict response from list_recommendations() instead of filtering each list inside the dict.

backend_ownership_filtering: GET /api/athletes - Director sees 25, Williams sees 13, Garcia sees 12 ✓
GET /api/athletes/{id} - Coach gets 403 for another coach's athlete, 200 for own ✓
GET /api/support-pods/{athlete_id} - Coach gets 403 for another coach's athlete ✓
POST /api/athletes/{id}/notes - Coach gets 403 for another coach's athlete ✓
frontend_ownership_ui: Coach Williams Mission Control shows Athletes: 13 ✓
Coach Williams Events page shows filtered events (6 upcoming) ✓
Coach Williams Advocacy page shows error due to backend bug ✗

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In /app/backend/routers/advocacy.py line 28-29, list_recommendations() returns a dict with structure {needs_attention: [], drafts: [], recently_sent: [], closed: [], stats: {}}. The code then calls filter_by_athlete_id(all_recs, current_user) which expects a list. When iterating over a dict, Python iterates over keys (strings), so item.get('athlete_id') fails because strings don't have .get() method.
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Change advocacy.py to filter each list in the response dict separately: filtered_result = {key: filter_by_athlete_id(items, current_user) if isinstance(items, list) else items for key, items in all_recs.items()}
